title: Incident Comms Approval Chain
description: An incident-and-change response workflow that coordinates mitigation, approvals, and execution sequencing under operational urgency. Focus case: incident comms approval chain.
assumptions: Approver and reviewer identities are pre-registered as candidate objects prior to workflow initiation.
Communication templates and distribution lists are validated and available in the system catalog.
Compliance artifacts, when required, can be attached and validated by designated authors before finalization.
Actions represent atomic workflow steps executed by automation or human operators with mutually exclusive responsibilities.
risks: Delayed approvals from on-call or executive approvers may cause missed mitigation windows.
Misbinding of services or components could lead to incorrect stakeholder notifications.
Insufficient validation of communication templates may result in non-compliant public messaging.
Race conditions in assignment and revocation steps could leave the case without an active approver.
